sourceMovie.title: Parkland
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Parkland for the same reason it broke through in 2013: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a drama rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Peter Landesman keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The history and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Dallas Buyers Club
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Parkland clicked for you, Dallas Buyers Club (2013) is a strong next stop: both trade in history and drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Parkland?
faq.answer: Try Dallas Buyers Club, Gangs of New York, Black Mass—each shares DNA with Parkland while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Parkland?
faq.answer: Parkland (2013) is best described as History, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Parkland worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this history lane.
